title: Best approach for Two Sum — HashMap vs Brute Force?
body: I tried brute force O(n²) first and it passes, but I want to understand the HashMap approach better. Can anyone break it down?

comments.body: The key insight: for each number x, you need (target - x). Store each number in a hashmap as you go. For every new number, check if its complement already exists. This gives you O(n) time and O(n) space.

title: Understanding DP vs Memoization — what's the real difference?
body: I keep seeing both terms used interchangeably but they seem different. Bottom-up vs top-down? Which is better?

title: Weekly Challenge: Graph traversal problems compilation
body: Sharing a list of graph problems I solved this week, from BFS/DFS basics to Dijkstra. Great for anyone starting graphs!

comments.body: Great compilation! I'd also add Number of Islands as a must-solve for BFS beginners.
